finding.title: SQL injection in get_user via f-string interpolation of user_id
finding.description: The /api/users/<user_id> handler interpolates the untrusted path parameter directly into the SQL string with no parameterization, allowing arbitrary SQL injection and full data exfiltration.

evidence.sink: cur.execute(f"SELECT id, email FROM users WHERE id = '{user_id}'")

finding.title: Server-side request forgery in fetch_preview
finding.description: The url query parameter is passed to requests.get without host allowlisting, letting an attacker reach internal services and cloud metadata.

finding.title: Hardcoded API credential in config module
finding.description: A long-lived API key is hardcoded as a module-level constant instead of being read from the environment or a secret store.

finding.title: Missing authentication on admin metrics endpoint
finding.description: The /admin/metrics route has no authentication decorator, so any unauthenticated caller can read internal metrics.

finding.title: SQL injection reachable from get_user id parameter
finding.description: Duplicate report of the unparameterized SQL query in the user lookup path; same sink, reported a few lines later.
